content: Devotional gatherings are spaces for people from all walks of life to partake of food for their soul. Often held in people’s homes, devotional gatherings are open to anyone to share prayers spoken, sung, or shared in other artistic forms.
summary: Devotional gatherings are spaces for people from all walks of life to partake of food for their soul. Open to anyone to share prayers spoken, sung, or shared in other artistic forms.
title: Devotionals

content: Baha’is believe that children are the most precious treasure a community can possess and thus place emphasis on their spiritual and moral development. Classes for the spiritual education of children are organized at the neighborhood level.
summary: Baha’is believe that children are the most precious treasure a community can possess, and thus place emphasis on their spiritual and moral development.

title: Special Events

content:
This page lists Baha’i events for Baha’is. Most Baha’i events are open to the public, but there are a handful of events attended only by Baha’is. These include Feasts, in which we carry out discussions of community governance, and the yearly unit conventions where deliberations take place at the national level.
What is a Baha’i? Informally, it’s anyone who calls themselves Baha’i. Actual membership (or enrollment, as it is called in the Baha’i community) is a formal declaration of acceptance of the mission of Baha’u’llah, the prophet and founder of the Baha’i Faith.
summary: Feasts, planning meetings, and other events for the Baha’i community. Click to learn more.
title: Baha'i Community Meetings

content:
The Baha’i Faith is a world-embracing community of believers following the teachings of Baha’u’llah, the prophet and founder of the Baha’i Faith. Please see https://www.bahai.us for detailed information about our beliefs and the organization of our communities.
This website is for the Westchester Baha’i Communities, a network of Baha’i assemblies, groups, and associated organizations throughout localities in Westchester County, New York. Postings are done on behalf of the organizers of the various events, whether they be official Baha’i governing agencies (LSAs), the initiatives of individual groups and members, or initiatives of Baha’i-inspired organizations.
This page is the work of individual initiative, in cooperation with the Baha’i Area Teaching Committee (ATC) for Westchester County, New York. The organizer, maintainer, and author of “general information” web pages is Joe Bradley (Northcastle township) and any mistakes and omissions are his own. Copyrights are the joint property of the Baha’i LSAs therein.
Credits and Attributions # The front page images are use-with-attribution artwork courtesy of the following:
Extracts and details from clipartmag.com:

title: About

content:
To contact the Baha’i communities in Westchester County (and elsewhere), please call:
1-800-22-UNITE
They will direct you to the Baha’i community nearest you.
Most events listed on this site have a contact person — rather than calling the general number, feel free to contact the event organizer directly. They will be happy to tell you about the event and anything else you might want to know.
title: Contact

content: Joint Zoom devotional hosted by the Mirafzali-Porooshasp families. Currently online. Contact atc@westchesterbahais.org for login information.
title: Friday Night Zoom Devotional

content:
Official Baha’i Websites # Baha’is of the United States Baha’i World Centre Baha’is of New York City Baha’i Reference Library — official repository for translated writings of the Baha’i Faith. Looking for West Chester, Pennsylvania? Main Line Baha’i Community is probably where you want to start. Other Baha’i Websites of Interest # Baha’i Teachings
Baha’i Blog — podcasts and more
Baha’i Library Online — critical website for Baha’i scholarship
Wilmette Institute — online courses in Baha’i-related subjects, open to anyone
“What is the Baha’i Faith?” — a popular YouTube video introducing the Baha’i Faith, voiceover by actor Rainn Wilson
The Internet # There is a lot of material on the internet about the Baha’i Faith. Much of it is by dedicated believers, though there are others. As with everything on the internet, caveat emptor. The websites above are accurate and reputable. For any subject — consider the source.
title: Links

content:
This weekly event is a series of community conversations open to the public. The goal is to meet people, make connections, and discuss activities and study circles leading to positive social action.
Register at the Mount Vernon Alive! website. Contact atc@westchesterbahais.org for URL and details.
title: Mount Vernon Alive

The New Era Creative Space (NECS) is a child and youth development organization inspiring social change through creative programs.
Visit the NECS website for more information.

Children’s classes are reconvening most Sunday mornings over Zoom. This year’s class caters to kindergarten through 4th grade.

For older children, youth activities are being planned — email atc@westchesterbahais.org for information on forming youth groups.
